title: DoorDash's Strategic Expansion Into Grocery Delivery: A Pathway to Long-Term Market Share and Revenue Diversification
text: DoorDash became the leading third-party marketplace in order volume across grocery and retail in the US by 2025, with the company having added thousands of new grocery stores to its marketplace by 2024. The grocery business gross order volume doubled year-over-year. DoorDash has over 150,000 grocery, retail, convenience and alcohol stores on the platform, up from 100,000 a year ago, offering over 11 million grocery and retail products. The company expanded partnerships to include major retailers like Kroger, Sephora, Tractor Supply, Dick's Sporting Goods, Raley's, Grocery Outlet, Sprouts, Giant Eagle, Weis Markets, DSW, Family Dollar, and Old Navy. In 2025, new partnerships included 33 additional grocery partners nationwide and multiple retail partners, broadening category reach. This strategic expansion into higher-margin, less labor-intensive verticals counterbalances low-margin food delivery and provides revenue diversification and reduced sensitivity to restaurant market fluctuations.
